statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.PivotTarget (MetricName, RedCount, BlackCount, SilverCount) SELECT 'product_counts', ISNULL([Red], 0), ISNULL([Black], 0), ISNULL([Silver], 0) FROM (SELECT Color, ProductID FROM bronze.Product) AS src PIVOT (COUNT(ProductID) FOR Color IN ([Red], [Black], [Silver])) AS p

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT 'product_counts' AS MetricName, ISNULL([Red], 0) AS RedCount, ISNULL([Black], 0) AS BlackCount, ISNULL([Silver], 0) AS SilverCount FROM ( SELECT Color, ProductID FROM [bronze].[Product] ) AS src PIVOT ( COUNT(ProductID) FOR Color IN ([Red], [Black], [Silver]) ) AS p
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import_product AS ( SELECT Color, ProductID FROM [bronze].[Product] ), pivoted AS ( SELECT [Red], [Black], [Silver] FROM import_product PIVOT ( COUNT(ProductID) FOR Color IN ([Red], [Black], [Silver]) ) AS p ), final AS ( SELECT 'product_counts' AS MetricName, ISNULL([Red], 0) AS RedCount, ISNULL([Black], 0) AS BlackCount, ISNULL([Silver], 0) AS SilverCount FROM pivoted ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries source from [bronze].[Product]. Extracted uses it directly in subquery, refactored imports it in first CTE.

output_columns.summary: Both return identical columns: MetricName (string literal 'product_counts'), RedCount (ISNULL INT), BlackCount (ISNULL INT), SilverCount (ISNULL INT).

joins.summary: No joins in either query. Both operate on single source table with PIVOT as sole transformation.

filters.summary: Neither query has explicit WHERE clauses. Both process all rows from bronze.Product.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both use COUNT(ProductID) aggregated by Color via PIVOT clause with identical pivot column list ([Red], [Black], [Silver]). ISNULL applied to handle null counts in both.
